channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: JD Vance: Donald Trump improved Obamacare
item.description: Sen. JD Vance defended Donald Trump's health care record, claiming Trump had improved the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KREX 5

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rips Walz, CBS moderators in debate's opening 15 minutes
item.description: “Walz is taking so many notes - Never seen a Candidate take more! He needs the notes to keep his brain intact,” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Kirk becomes a hurricane as North Carolina reels from Helene
item.description: Desperate residents of the storm-battered mountains of western North Carolina lined up for water and food, hunted for cellphone signals and slogged buckets from creeks to flush toilets days after Hurricane Helene’s remnants deluged the region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search crews with cadaver dogs wade through muck of communities ‘wiped off the map’ by Helene
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris in the mountains of western North Carolina on Tuesday looking for more victims of Hurricane Helene days after the storm carved a deadly and destructive path through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, JEFF AMY and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Explainer: How immigrants end up on ICE's non-detained docket
item.description: Recent reports that over 435,000 convicted criminals are on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement's non-detained docket (NDD) -- meaning they are not detained by the agency while they await immigration proceedings -- has spurred much criticism and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: “Peter wasn’t fired for something he said; he was fired for something he couldn’t say,” ADF senior counsel Tyson Langhofer said.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of missing mother Emily Strite found in Missouri
item.description: Emily Strite was last seen on April 12, 2024, and reported missing on April 20, 2024, according to the Jefferson County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: The US could see shortages and higher retail prices if a dockworkers strike drags on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977. Workers began walking picket lines early Tuesday, picketing near ports all along the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education a potential flashpoint in Vance-Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Education hardly came up in last month’s Trump-Harris presidential debate, but it could be a central focus at Tuesday’s undercard event.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Is the food in the fridge still good? California wants to end the guessing game
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California wants to help end the everyday household debate over whether the food in the fridge is still good to eat. Food labels that say “sell by” or “best before” are misleading because they have no universal meaning under current laws.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Habit Burger & Grill trolls rival In-N-Out
item.description: Habit Burger & Grill isn’t letting anyone, especially In-N-Out Burger, forget that it has the best fast-food burger in town—at least according to one survey.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KTLA 5

item.title: Tennessee migrant workers arrested for allegedly looting Hurricane Helene devastation
item.description: Washington County, Tennessee, authorities arrested eight migrants accused of looting residential homes badly damaged by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Publix donates $1 million to support disaster relief following Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida-based grocery chain, Publix, announced it's making a major contribution to help relief efforts in areas affected by the catastrophic damage left behind by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, ABC 4

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: New Hampshire parents sue school district for banning them over 'silent protest' against trans soccer player
item.description: A group of New Hampshire parents filed a lawsuit against a school district over their removal from a girls soccer game for protesting against a transgender player.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, KRON4

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump suggests 'one rough hour' of policing will end theft
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Donald Trump has suggested that “one rough hour” of law enforcement action would tamp down retail theft, an echo of his longstanding support for more aggressive and potentially violent policing.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Helene's death toll rises to more than 100 as supplies rushed to isolated communities
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 100.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, singer-songwriter and actor, dies at 88
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and an A-list Hollywood actor, has died. Kristofferson died at his home on Maui, Hawaii, on Saturday, family spokeswoman Ebie McFarland said in an email.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Supplies arrive by plane and mule as Helene death toll passes 100
item.description: Desperate residents isolated by washed-out roads and the lack of power and cellular service in western North Carolina lined up Monday for fresh water and a chance to message loved ones that they were alive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how CM Punk is helping WWE NXT's Roxanne Perez
item.description: CM Punk, in particular, has become a significant influence in her career, mentoring her through her rise in NXT.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, ABC 4
